html:
RECOUP Health Longevity Optimization Program to lower biological age

According to 2020 data, the average Indian lives only 3640 weeks (70 years). Several of these 70 years are spent in poor health, with a burden of mostly preventable diseases.

Wouldn't 5200 weeks (100 years) of life with more healthy years be better? Is it achievable?

In the era of precision medicine, genomics, biometrics, targeted blood work, digital therapeutics, nutraceuticals, therapies, medicines, and technology allow us to assess and improve an individual's biological age. This allows us to offer personalized and preventive interventions that can optimize the longevity of good health.

However, you may not receive most of these services from your usual healthcare provider. Conventional thinking only addresses a problem when it presents itself, but this approach may not provide a complete diagnosis until it's too late. Symptoms often appear when an underlying condition has already developed for months or even years.

Now, you can start adding more health each day of your life and add more healthy years to your age. Self-enroll in Recoup Health's Longevity Optimization Program (RHLOP) to:

  1. Delay age-related disorders by sustaining youthful functional capacities for more years
  2. Detect functional disorders/declines early and postpone the onset of non-communicable diseases (NCDs)
  3. Get more from preventive health checks beyond just lab tests
  4. Prevent chronicity of diseases by compressing sickness and illness periods
  5. Prevent overdependence on medicines and surgeries using personal data to inform decisions on appropriate use and timely discontinuation of medicines
  6. Overcome ignorance and gain from longevity competencies and practices.

Preventive care is limited to taking proactive measures to prevent illness.

Optimization of healthy life is all about achieving the best possible health using evidence-based scientific methods, sustaining incremental benefits from:

  • a.Fitness of all organ systems
  • b.Physical and mental agility
  • c.Resilient mind to have wise choices
  • d.Ability to derive life satisfaction
  • e.Sustain independence in each changing phase of life

RHLOP's New Age Expanded and User-friendly Clinical Spectrum of Care:

RLOP simplifies your longevity needs by integrating the principles of longevity sciences, geroscience, geriatric medicine, and functional and lifestyle medicine to:

  • Understanding the ageing process and ways it drives disease development
  • Finding precise clinical interventions to:
    • Optimize cellular functions constantly interacting with our environments and influenced by our choices, functions and activities.
    • Eliminate aged cells in clinically appropriate ways, reducing the risk of degenerative diseases and cancers.
    • Improve structural integrity and intrinsic functional capacities across all 12 organ systems throughout the ageing process.
    • Build biological reserves and gain physiological resilience to withstand stress and strain constantly faced in life through all human development and advancement phases.
    • Overcoming all preventable harms, injuries, limitations, and diseases that otherwise rise in incidence and prevalence.

Thereby extending the human lifespan while maintaining overall health and well-being across all age groups.

Lower your biological age by switching to RHLOP now. Make the best of your health opportunities:

8 Biological and Functional age assessment tracks:

  • Anthropometric Age: 12 parameters to check the ageing effect on body composition, constitution, posture and gait
  • Physical function index (for calendar age under 50 years): Assessing performance of over 12 classes of exercises covering various joints, muscle groups and bones
  • Frailty index (for calendar age 50 years and over): Assessing over 34 parametrized activities covering various joints, muscle groups, and bones.
  • Cognitive function index: 24-parameter assessment for brain functions of daily needs to lead a productive life and prevent degenerative neurological diseases.
  • Hormonal Age: Multidimensional assessment of various hormones' functional status and objective lab values.
  • Genetic ageing: Telomere test plus a personalized mix from among 14 epigenetic markers to determine the pace of ageing.
  • Facial and Skin ageing: 20 parameter assessment for the ageing effect of facial and general skin.
  • Vascular ageing: 10 parameter assessment for ageing effect on blood vessels, risk of heart and brain diseases.
  • Organ ageing: Multiparameter evaluation for ageing effect on heart, lungs, liver, kidney, bones, brain and special organs like eyes, ears and teeth.

8 Annual health check tracks include:

  • a.Health scores with recommendations
  • b.Biometric health screens
  • c.Wellness medical examination
  • d.Telomere age test
  • e.Epigenetic markers evaluation
  • f.Microbiome studies
  • g.Longevity lab tests (Basic)
  • h.Specialized lab test panels

Annual review of previous year's achievements and prognostics followed by proactive, precise, goal-oriented planning for the upcoming year.

Annualized longevity optimization care plan:

  • a.Longevity skills training and personal practices: Mindful meditation, mindful movements, coherence training, centenarian practices, minimalism lifestyle practices, healthy circadian clock, tech use optimization, home ergonomics, health inventory at home/office/travel, happiness, physician-enabled self-care and home-based detox protocol, caregiver training, and color therapy.
  • b.Longevity supplements and medicines; Cannabinoids; bioidentical hormones.
  • c.Wellness challenges with incremental levels of challenges using interactive biofeedback systems and Augmented Reality/ Virtual Reality experience sessions.
  • d.Resilience building programs: Annualized 4 weeks to 18 weeks duration curated for each enrolled member, optimizing stress response by each of the 12 resiliencies vital for all humans
  • e.Regenerative therapies: Annualized mix of curated therapies featuring colon hydrotherapy, system detox, medical massages, Infrared sauna, cryotherapy, aquatic therapy, PEMF, vagus nerve stimulation, neurogenic boost, IV nutrients, Peptides, Ozone therapy, Hydrogen therapy, yoga, physiotherapy and occupational therapies.
  • f.Substance dependence reduction program: Digital detox, smoking cessation, alcohol use optimization, de-addiction support.
  • g.Longevity-related health programs: Nutrigenomics-based diet plans; Metabolic autophagy, Polypharmacy reconciliation; Social support systems; Annual wellness champion leagues
  • h.Longevity journal with coaching support to capture memories, micro-experiences, micro-celebrations, and creative pursuits to meet daily health needs, inculcate habitual adherence to longevity practices and manage chronic conditions thereby crossing health milestones

RHLOP Team:

  • Functional Medicine Physicians
  • Lifestyle Medicine Physicians
  • Rehabilitation Physicians
  • Other Specialist Physicians
  • Ayurveda Physicians
  • Naturopathy and Yoga Physicians
  • Nutritionists
  • Clinical Psychologists
  • Physiotherapists
  • Occupational Therapists
  • Aquatic Therapists
  • Health coaches
  • Nurses
  • Other professionals, as required
  • Patient Care Coordinators

RHLOP Alliance and Integration Program:

  • Corporate health program: Resilient employees for productive work-life transformation
  • Wellness service providers: Health benefit plans; Wellness challenge leagues (Onsite)
  • Resorts: Longevity-based wellness retreats; Wellness challenge leagues (Offsite)
  • Insurance service providers: Health risk reversal
  • Institutional care: Hospitals; Academies; Research bodies
  • Residential welfare associations: Resilient residents and caring communities

Book your slot:

Webinar series 90-minute session (60-minute learning followed by 30 min Q & A):

  • Why do we age?
  • How do we age?
  • How to gain from the RHLOP program?

Schedule your facility tour:

Get RHLOP experience

Previsit: Questionnaire-based Assessment and Recommendation

3-hour version (Snack provided):

  • Biometric Cardiometabolic check (30 minutes)
  • 1 therapy of 45 mins (Panchakarma, Aquatic therapy, Polyvagal stimulation, AR/VR experience, Exergaming).
  • A 15-minute session with 2 of either a Nutritionist, Psychologist, Yoga Therapist, Physiotherapist
  • A 15-minute session with a Physician

6-hour version (Snack provided):

  • Two biometric tests from either of the Biometric Cardiometabolic check (30 minutes); Medical Infrared Thermography (30 minutes); Foot analysis (10 mins); HRV session (20 mins).
  • There can be up to 2 therapies, each lasting a maximum of 45 minutes (Panchakarma, Aquatic therapy, Polyvagal stimulation, AR/VR experience, Exergaming).
  • A 15-minute session Nutritionist, Psychologist, Yoga therapist, Physiotherapist &
  • A 15-minute session with a Physician
